message.content:
The OWASP Top 10:2021 categories are **A01:2021 through A10:2021**, and OWASP’s 2021 introduction page confirms the exact category names and the main scope changes. For audit use, the clearest canonical way to check each category is to verify whether the application exhibits the risks described by OWASP’s category page and the mapped CWEs. [2][6]

| Code | Name | What an auditor checks/tests for | Primary mapped CWE(s) | Canonical OWASP URL |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **A01:2021** | **Broken Access Control** | Tests whether users can act outside their intended privileges, including unauthorized direct object access, forced browsing, privilege escalation, method tampering, and horizontal/vertical access-control bypasses. [2] | **CWE-22, CWE-23, CWE-36, CWE-59, CWE-200, CWE-201, CWE-219, CWE-250, CWE-525, CWE-566, CWE-639, CWE-651, CWE-668, CWE-706, CWE-862, CWE-863, CWE-913, CWE-922** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021-Broken_Access_Control/` |
| **A02:2021** | **Cryptographic Failures** | Tests whether sensitive data is protected in transit and at rest, whether strong crypto is used correctly, whether passwords are stored safely, and whether keys, certificates, and cryptographic modes are properly managed. [2] | **CWE-259, CWE-320, CWE-328, CWE-331, CWE-347, CWE-523, CWE-532, CWE-622, CWE-798** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021-Cryptographic_Failures/` |
| **A03:2021** | **Injection** | Tests whether untrusted input can alter commands, queries, templates, expressions, or interpreter behavior, including SQL/NoSQL/LDAP/OS/ORM injection and XSS as part of this category in 2021. [2] | **CWE-77, CWE-78, CWE-79, CWE-89, CWE-90, CWE-93, CWE-94, CWE-95, CWE-96, CWE-98, CWE-99, CWE-116, CWE-117, CWE-1336** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021-Injection/` |
| **A04:2021** | **Insecure Design** | Tests for missing or weak security controls in the design itself, such as absent threat modeling, unsafe trust assumptions, missing abuse-case handling, weak rate limiting, and lack of secure design patterns. [2] | **CWE-1008, CWE-1173, CWE-1336** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A04_2021-Insecure_Design/` |
| **A05:2021** | **Security Misconfiguration** | Tests for insecure defaults, overly permissive settings, unnecessary features, missing hardening, exposed error details, misconfigured headers/CORS/cloud storage, and incomplete patch/hardening baselines. [2] | **CWE-2, CWE-16, CWE-526, CWE-537, CWE-541, CWE-611, CWE-776, CWE-942, CWE-1004, CWE-1137** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A05_2021-Security_Misconfiguration/` |
| **A06:2021** | **Vulnerable and Outdated Components** | Tests whether the application uses libraries, frameworks, containers, runtimes, or platform components with known vulnerabilities, unsupported versions, or no inventory/SBOM discipline. [2] | **CWE-1104, CWE-937** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A06_2021-Vulnerable_and_Outdated_Components/` |
| **A07:2021** | **Identification and Authentication Failures** | Tests login, session, credential, and identity flows for weak passwords, credential stuffing resistance, MFA gaps, session fixation, session timeout issues, and broken account recovery/identity proofing. [2] | **CWE-287, CWE-288, CWE-290, CWE-294, CWE-295, CWE-297, CWE-300, CWE-346, CWE-384, CWE-522, CWE-613, CWE-640, CWE-798, CWE-287** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A07_2021-Identification_and_Authentication_Failures/` |
| **A08:2021** | **Software and Data Integrity Failures** | Tests whether code, updates, plugins, serialized data, CI/CD artifacts, and integrity-critical pipelines are verified before use, and whether unsigned/untrusted content can influence execution. [2] | **CWE-353, CWE-426, CWE-494, CWE-502, CWE-565, CWE-829** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A08_2021-Software_and_Data_Integrity_Failures/` |
| **A09:2021** | **Security Logging and Monitoring Failures** | Tests whether security-relevant events are logged, protected, correlated, alerted on, and retained; also whether failed logins, privilege changes, and suspicious actions are detectable and actionable. [2] | **CWE-223, CWE-224, CWE-532, CWE-778, CWE-779, CWE-117, CWE-712** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A09_2021-Security_Logging_and_Monitoring_Failures/` |
| **A10:2021** | **Server-Side Request Forgery** | Tests whether the server can be induced to make attacker-controlled outbound requests, including to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, and arbitrary URLs, and whether URL allowlisting, parsing, and network egress controls prevent it. [2] | **CWE-918** [2] | `https://owasp.org/Top10/A10_2021-Server-Side_Request_Forgery_(SSRF)/` |

A small note on the URLs: OWASP’s canonical category pages use the `Top10/A0x_2021-.../` pattern; the A10 page includes the SSRF label in the path. The names and category codes above match OWASP’s 2021 introduction page exactly. [2][6]

If you want, I can also convert this into a CSV-style catalog, JSON, or a shorter auditor checklist per row.
